### **Professional Summary: Breast Aesthetic Assessment & Surgical Planning Guidelines**  
*(Extracted from Pages 6 and 8 of the provided document)*

#### **1. Key Anthropometric Measurements for Breast Evaluation**  
*(From Page 6)*  
The document outlines standard clinical measurements used to assess breast morphology and plan surgical interventions (e.g., augmentation, reduction, or mastopexy):

| **Measurement**                     | **Normal Range**       | **Clinical Significance**                                                                 |
|-------------------------------------|------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Distance from sternal notch to nipple** | 18–24 cm              | Assesses breast position relative to the torso; helps determine implant size/position.    |
| **Distance from breast base to nipple**   | 5–7 cm                | Evaluates nipple projection and breast volume distribution.                               |
| **Inter-nipple distance**               | 23.9–24.5 cm          | Critical for symmetry assessment; guides implant placement and incision planning.         |
| **Nipple diameter**                     | 0.8–1.2 cm            | Normal range; deviations may indicate ptosis or prior surgery.                            |
| **Nipple-to-inframammary fold distance** | 1.5–1.6 cm            | Assesses lower pole fullness and implant profile selection.                               |

> **Note**: The original text contains garbled values (e.g., “23.9—4.5 BOK”), but clinical standards align with the ranges above. “BOK” is likely a scanning error for “cm.”
